name: roundtrip_external_body_trusted
reason: `RepeatN::theorem_serialize_parse_roundtrip` is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()` — the fundamental roundtrip property is trusted without proof, despite a verified helper existing

name: prefix_secure_helper_external_body
reason: `lemma_prefix_secure_helper` is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()` — prefix security for RepeatN is trusted and used as a critical dependency in the roundtrip helper proof

name: serialize_empty_is_empty
reason: Serializing an empty sequence should produce empty bytes — if non-empty output were produced for zero elements, the fold_left would have injected phantom data

name: wf_enforces_length_equals_count
code:
proof fn phi_4_wf_enforces_length_equals_count<C: SecureSpecCombinator>(c: &RepeatN<C>, vs: Seq<C::Type>)
    requires
        c.wf(vs),
    ensures
        vs.len() == c.1,
{
}
reason: Well-formedness requires `vs.len() == self.1` — if wf accepted sequences of different lengths, the parse-serialize roundtrip would break since parse always returns exactly `n` elements

name: serialize_ignores_count_field
reason: `spec_serialize` uses `fold_left` over `vs` without referencing the `usize` count field — serialization is completely independent of the repetition count, so `RepeatN(c, 0).spec_serialize(vs)` serializes all elements regardless
